sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.85 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let$F$be a free pro-$p 0.2ex$-group with a basis$X$and let$R=r^F$be the closed normal subgroup generated by an element$r$. We say that an element$s$of$F$is asso\-ci\-at\-ed to$r$\/ if$s^F=R$. [r]{a)} Suppose that none of the elements associated to$r$is a$p$th power of an element in$F$. Is it true that$F/R$is torsion-free? [r]{b)} Among the elements associated to$r$there is one that depends on the ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
